macro_summary: Markets staged a dramatic quarter-end rally on March 31, with SPY surging to $650.34 (+3.1%) and QQQ jumping to $577.18 (+3.8%), per Yahoo Finance — the Dow rose roughly 1,000 points as investors grew hopeful about potential de-escalation in the Iran conflict, per Yahoo Finance reporting. The SOXX semiconductor ETF surged 7.2% (using fast_info headline; history shows +6.1% — a minor data conflict, both directionally consistent) to $328.66, marking a sharp single-session bounce after four days of TurboQuant-driven selling, though the ETF remains roughly 18% below its pre-TurboQuant peak. VIX dropped sharply to 25.25, though the reported -17.5% daily change is inconsistent with the 5-day change of -6.3% — this appears to be a data artifact; the directional move toward lower fear is consistent with the equity rally, but the precise magnitude is unreliable. Non-Farm Payrolls for March are due April 3, and CPI follows April 10 — both will test whether the Fed's higher-for-longer stance holds.
commodities_note: Gold shows a data conflict: fast_info reports a prior close of $4,584.90 implying a +2.8% daily gain to $4,713.60, while history data implies a prior close of $4,526.00 for a +4.1% gain — using the fast_info figure as the headline, gold appears to have extended its all-time high run, up roughly 2.8% on the day. The 5-day and 1-month trajectories confirm the bullish trend regardless of which prior-close is correct. Gold at all-time highs with no attractive entry (likely well above its 50-day average) — no action warranted until a pullback to a defined support level. WTI held near $103, with the -0.03% daily change essentially flat — oil remains structurally elevated on Hormuz disruption now entering day 23, with the $100-135/bbl analyst range still in play per prior reporting. DXY weakened -0.69% to 99.76, a modest dollar pullback consistent with the risk-on session. The 10-year Treasury yield eased to 4.31%, slightly below the 4.34% level from March 31.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. DBS is sitting just 0.4% above its 50-day average of SGD 57.37, which is itself a tight invalidation — the risk/reward pipeline flags this: structural support at SGD 57.00 (1.0% below) is the more meaningful thesis test. Invalidation at SGD 57.37 (0.4% below) is within normal intraday noise; tight stop — normal intraday volatility may trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use SGD 57.00 (1.0% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The nearest resistance at SGD 58.29 gives a 3.0:1 R:R on paper, but the denominator is so thin that sizing must be minimal. NIM compression from the rate-pause cycle remains the structural headwind per the Singapore cluster narrative (day 22), and analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 8.8% — a modest but real drag. A 5.4% dividend yield at 1.1x book is the primary reason to own this, not price appreciation. The PEG of 4.64 reflects compressed growth, not value. Volume was low, offering no confirmation. Bottom line: DBS pays you to wait, but there's no compelling entry catalyst right now — the thesis doesn't break unless SGD 57.00 gives way, and it doesn't upgrade until NIM pressure eases or US10Y breaks below 4.20%.

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — OCBC is 5.9% above its 50-day average of SGD 21.23 with RSI at 71.1 (overbought), and the pipeline has issued a hard entry block on both grounds; the R:R deteriorated to 0.9:1 (derived quality), which is unfavourable and disqualifies ACCUMULATE. OCBC has rallied strongly over the past month (+6.5%) and the 5-day gain of 5.1% extends the extension further. Resistance at SGD 23.60 is the next meaningful level, while the invalidation at SGD 21.23 (50-day SMA, 5.6% below) means the downside risk now exceeds the 5.0% upside to resistance. Volume was below average, confirming no institutional conviction behind this move. Earnings growth at 2.7% and a 3.82% yield are supportive of a hold thesis but not a stretch entry at current levels. Bottom line: OCBC is too extended for a fresh entry — wait for a pullback toward SGD 21.50-22.00 before reconsidering.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — UOB has recovered slightly and is now just 1.2% below its 50-day average of SGD 37.42, with RSI at 52.6 in neutral-bullish territory, no longer warranting CAUTION designation. However, the R:R is deeply unfavourable at 0.3:1: the nearest resistance at SGD 37.40 is only 1.1% above current price while invalidation sits at the 200-day SMA of SGD 35.59 (3.8% below), yielding a skewed setup. Analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 6.7% — a material headwind that keeps this as a HOLD rather than anything more constructive. Revenue growth is negative at -1.8%. The 26.6% discount to cluster median PE is notable value, but NIM compression (now in day 22 of the Singapore cluster weakness narrative) continues to suppress earnings power. Bottom line: UOB is no longer technically broken, but the value case needs either NIM stabilisation or a US10Y break below 4.20% to convert to action — neither condition is near.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. SIA sits exactly at its 50-day average of SGD 6.67 with an R:R ratio that the pipeline flags as distorted (N/A — at or below invalidation), meaning there is no meaningful upside edge at the current entry point. The Wall Street Journal reported on March 30 that oil is rising on fears of a widening Middle East conflict — with WTI near $103, fuel cost headwinds remain real for an airline. Analyst consensus expects earnings to decline 63.4%, which is severe; per the earnings decline prominence rule, this is a material concern that must weigh heavily on the signal. Revenue growth is just 1.9%, yet the stock trades at 17.5x forward PE — a 25.9% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.9x — without a clear growth justification given the earnings trajectory. The 8.2% FCF yield is genuinely strong and the 5.3% dividend is attractive, but these are insufficient to offset the earnings risk and the geopolitical fuel cost overhang. Bottom line: SIA's structural cash generation is real, but a 63% expected earnings decline and rising fuel costs make this unattractive until there is Hormuz de-escalation or earnings guidance stabilises.

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Singtel is 2.7% above its 50-day average of SGD 4.89 with RSI neutral at 52.6 — slightly extended but within the acceptable range for monitoring. Bottom line: Singtel's trend is intact but valuation is stretched and the R:R is unattractive at current levels — wait for a pullback toward SGD 4.89-5.00 to improve the setup.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to ACCUMULATE — all eight ACCUMULATE gates pass: caution_source is base_scorer, R:R is 2.2:1 (observed quality, not distorted), thesis intact, no earnings within 5 days (May 21), VIX at 25.25 (below 30), invalidation at $169.81 is specific and quantifiable, and rr_distorted is false. ACCUMULATE gate check: all mechanical gates pass for NVDA. No ticker-level thesis break identified. This is the first ACCUMULATE signal for NVDA this session. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation. Invalidation at $169.81 (nearest support, 2.6% below); if this breaks, the rally is a dead-cat bounce and the thesis resets. Structural support at $164.30 (5.8% below) is the more meaningful thesis test. Upside target $184.47 (nearest resistance, 5.8% above) gives 2.2:1 R:R. Volume confirms the move — above average on today's +6.65% session. The Blackwell Ultra ramp thesis remains intact per NVDA's Q4 FY2026 earnings call: Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B implies the revenue ramp analysts were modeling for Q3 has been pulled forward. CoreWeave secured $8.5B in GPU-backed financing per Yahoo Finance, confirming enterprise AI demand is real. At 15.7x forward PE — a 16.9% discount to the Semis cluster median — NVDA is not expensive for 73% revenue growth and 95.6% earnings growth expected. PEG of 1.0 is fair. The FCF yield of 1.37% is low, meaning the thesis depends on continued earnings expansion. The 50-day SMA at $182.81 is declining, which is a headwind, but the R:R calculation already prices this in. The TurboQuant narrative remains a risk — if Google's algorithm genuinely displaces GPU-centric architecture, this thesis weakens — but today's 7.2% SOXX surge and CoreWeave financing suggest the market is reassessing that fear. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Bottom line: NVDA is the cheapest quality name in semis relative to its growth rate, and fear about TurboQuant may be overdone — enter a starter position at $170-175, stop below $169.81, add on a 50-day SMA reclaim with volume.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to ACCUMULATE — all eight ACCUMULATE gates pass: caution_source is base_scorer, R:R is 2.1:1 (observed quality, not distorted), thesis intact with confirmed catalysts, no earnings within 5 days (May 6), VIX at 25.25 (below 30), invalidation at $195.96 is specific, and rr_distorted is false. ACCUMULATE gate check: all mechanical gates pass for AMD. No ticker-level thesis break identified. PAPER TRADE — under evaluation. Invalidation at $195.96 (200-day SMA, 3.7% below) — a break here confirms the thesis is broken. Upside target $218.84 (nearest resistance, 7.6% above) gives 2.1:1 R:R. Volume was above average today on the +4.97% move. The Meta $100B+ GPU supply deal (per AMD IR press release, Feb 24, 2026 — confirmed source) is the single most important thesis driver: up to 6GW of AMD Instinct GPUs deployed through 2030, with first 1GW shipments in H2 2026 directly validating AMD as a credible NVIDIA alternative at hyperscale. Bloomberg reported South Korean AI startup Upstage is reportedly in talks to purchase 10,000 MI355 accelerators, adding further demand signal. The PEG of 0.52 with 34.1% revenue growth and 217.1% expected earnings growth is compelling — AMD trades at exactly cluster median (18.9x forward PE) but grows far faster than peers. Gaming revenue deceleration (per Zacks, March 31) is a near-term drag but does not change the AI accelerator thesis. The FCF yield of 1.38% is low, confirming the thesis depends on earnings delivery. ACCUMULATE — starter position. Bottom line: AMD is the most attractively valued AI GPU alternative on the watchlist given its growth rate and the confirmed Meta deal — enter $195-203, stop below $195.96, and let the H2 2026 Meta shipment milestone be the first thesis confirmation.

INTC.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 44x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Intel's 18A yield improvement story (confirmed by John Pitzer per overclock3d.net reporting) is the entire fundamental thesis, but the stock faces a 44.5x forward PE — a 135.4% premium to the Semis cluster median of 18.9x — with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and negative FCF yield (-2.03%). That combination fails the fundamental overlay badly: revenue is less than half the forward PE, FCF is negative, and the pipeline has issued a soft block requiring explicit earnings growth justification. The 18A foundry ramp with Apple reportedly engaged (per wccftech.com) and NVIDIA looking at 18A/14A for future Feynman chips provides optionality, but these are sampling/engagement phase signals — not firm orders. Earnings on April 24 (23 days away) represent a binary catalyst. R:R is 1.8:1 (observed) to nearest resistance — below the 2.0 threshold needed for ACCUMULATE, and the caution_source entries preclude it anyway. The 200-day SMA at $35.00 is 20.7% below — a long way to fall if the foundry thesis disappoints. Bottom line: Intel's foundry pivot is compelling in theory, but negative FCF, 44x multiple with no earnings growth, and a hard entry block make this untouchable until April 24 earnings show proof of 18A customer commitments converting to revenue.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML reports Q1 2026 results on April 15 (14 days away) — this is the single most important near-term catalyst for the entire Semis cluster, as per 24/7 Wall St., every quarterly print from the world's sole EUV manufacturer carries outsized weight for the AI chip supply chain. At 5.3% below its 50-day SMA of $1,394.56 (declining), the stock is technically broken but not deeply oversold (RSI 45.8). R:R is 2.4:1 (observed) to $1,410 resistance — above the 2.0 threshold, but ACCUMULATE is disqualified because caution_source is base_scorer AND the earnings proximity rule applies: entering ahead of a binary earnings event within 14 days (technically 7-day rule doesn't apply here, but 14-day proximity raises risk). The PEG of 1.92 and 30.6x forward PE (a 61.9% premium to cluster median) with only 4.9% revenue growth and 7.2% earnings growth is expensive — revenue growth is less than a sixth of the forward PE. ASML's backing of European AI data centers per Simply Wall St. shows strategic diversification beyond core lithography, but this is a long-dated optionality play. The 200-day SMA at $1,044.35 is a remarkable 20.9% below — structural support in a worst case. Invalidation at $1,283.60 (nearest support, 2.8% below). Bottom line: ASML is attractively positioned for long-term AI capex spending but expensive relative to near-term growth — wait for the April 15 earnings print before entering; a beat could convert this to WATCH or better.

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Micron has collapsed 16.3% below its 50-day SMA of $403.75 — a severe technical breakdown — with RSI at 36.6, approaching oversold territory but not yet there. The stock is down 14.6% in five days and 18.1% in a month, driven by the Google TurboQuant algorithm announcement raising fears about GPU-centric hyperscaler demand for HBM memory. Yahoo Finance noted memory stocks continued sliding on TurboQuant fears. The R:R here is 1.4:1 (derived quality — support/resistance are estimated from SMA levels, not observed swing points), which is below the 2.0 threshold for ACCUMULATE, and rr_quality being 'derived' independently disqualifies ACCUMULATE regardless. The valuation picture is extraordinary: 3.4x forward PE with 196% revenue growth and 756% expected earnings growth — the PEG of 0.18 appears anomalous and should be treated with caution (verify earnings growth estimate before relying on it). Analyst consensus is strong_buy. The invalidation at the 200-day SMA of $240.42 is 28.8% below — a very wide stop that makes position sizing treacherous. Motley Fool on April 1 noted the AI memory crunch thesis remains valid but questions its duration. Bottom line: Micron's valuation is deeply compelling if the HBM demand thesis survives TurboQuant scrutiny, but the technical breakdown is severe and the R:R setup uses estimated levels — wait for RSI to bottom below 30 and a base to form above the 100-day SMA of $334.82 before considering entry.

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Amazon is 3.5% below its declining 50-day SMA of $215.84 with RSI at 48.1 — technically not oversold, just stuck in a downtrend. The R:R is exactly 1.0:1 (observed) to nearest resistance at $219.43, which is symmetrically unattractive: equal upside and downside risk does not justify entry. The $200B AI capex commitment (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call and 10-K) is the thesis driver — AWS is accelerating and Amazon is developing its own Trainium chips — but the earnings growth consensus of just 5.0% is concerning given the capex burden. Amazon's satellite deal with Delta for Kuiper internet service (per Yahoo Finance, March 31) adds diversification but is not a near-term earnings catalyst. The PEG of 1.97 is expensive relative to 13.6% revenue growth and 5% earnings growth; FCF yield of 1.06% is low. The 200-day SMA at $224.59 sits above current price — structurally, Amazon is below both its major moving averages, which is a meaningful technical deterioration. Bottom line: Amazon's cloud and AI buildout is a compelling long-term story, but 1.0:1 R:R and a declining trend argue for patience — actionable below $197 at the observed support level, or on a 50-day SMA reclaim with volume confirmation.

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to CAUTION — Google's R:R has deteriorated sharply: invalidation at the 200-day SMA of $263.98 (8.0% below) but upside to nearest resistance at $291.53 is just 1.6%, giving a deeply unfavourable 0.2:1 R:R. Despite today's +5.57% rally on war de-escalation hopes, GOOG remains 7.84% below its declining 50-day SMA of $311.25, and RSI at 40.3 is still neutral-bearish. The TurboQuant narrative creates a specific company-level tension: Google is simultaneously the source of the algorithm disruption AND the primary AI cloud revenue engine — TPU v7 rentals to Meta, Anthropic, and Apple validate Google Cloud strength, but TurboQuant raises questions about whether the same company is cannibalising the GPU-centric capex that drives the rest of the watchlist. Needham reiterated Buy with a $400 price target per Insider Monkey (March 31), implying 39% upside from current levels. Analyst consensus expects 31.1% earnings growth at 21.4x forward PE — fair to slightly cheap on a growth-adjusted basis (PEG 1.68). But until the TurboQuant architectural implications are clearer from April 24 earnings (23 days away), this is an avoid. Bottom line: Google's business is strong and potentially undervalued, but the 0.2:1 R:R to nearest resistance means the stock needs to reclaim $311 before a clean entry exists — wait for the 50-day SMA reclaim.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Lumentum is 18.8% above its 50-day SMA of $591.55 — extreme extension territory — with the pipeline assigning a hard_block (caution_source: hard_block), which cannot be overridden. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed per NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion on March 9 are the structural drivers. Lumentum expanded its Greensboro, NC indium phosphide fab for AI lasers per Simply Wall St. (March 31), backed by hundreds of millions in planned investment — this validates the manufacturing buildout thesis. Despite today's +10.76% bounce, LITE is still down 12.4% in five days from the TurboQuant-driven selloff. The PEG of 0.14 appears anomalous given a 46.9x forward PE — verify before relying on it. FCF yield is -0.04%, meaning the thesis is entirely dependent on future earnings growth materialising from the AI optics ramp. The R:R is 0.7:1 (upside to $783.80 vs. downside to $591.55 SMA) — unfavourable even setting aside the hard block. Bottom line: Lumentum's photonics thesis is real and the NVIDIA partnership is confirmed, but 18.8% above its 50-day average with negative FCF makes this untouchable — wait for a pullback toward $600-620 before considering a position.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

COHR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — Coherent has pulled back to sit just 0.05% above its rising 50-day SMA of $238.09 (RSI 47.2, neutral-bearish), and the pipeline's caution_source is now absent (no hard_block, no base_scorer flag). However, the R:R is distorted (rr_distorted: true) — the invalidation at $238.09 is just 0.1% below current price (tight_invalidation: true), creating a mathematically extreme 38:1 ratio that is meaningless. Structural support at $230.15 (3.4% below) is the real risk level. The NVIDIA $2B strategic investment into Coherent for silicon photonics (confirmed per NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and the S&P 500 inclusion are confirmed catalysts. At 32.1x forward PE vs. cluster median of 39.5x, Coherent trades at an 18.7% cluster discount — relatively attractive. The 73% expected earnings growth is strong. Tight stop — normal intraday volatility will trigger invalidation before the thesis is tested; use $230.15 (3.4% below) as the mental stop. Bottom line: Coherent has pulled back to a key level and the NVIDIA partnership is confirmed — this is approaching an interesting entry, but wait for a clean close above $240 with above-average volume before committing.

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. XLE is 11.6% above its 50-day SMA of $54.88 with RSI at 71.0 (overbought) — the pipeline has issued a hard entry block on both extension and RSI grounds. Multiple MT Newswires updates on March 31 showed energy stocks fell in afternoon trading despite pre-market strength, consistent with profit-taking at extended levels. PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 78x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. Palantir sits just 0.95% below its declining 50-day SMA of $147.69 — close to a reclaim — but the pipeline has issued a hard entry block on valuation grounds: 78.3x forward PE is the highest on the watchlist and 252.7% above the BigTech cluster median of 22.2x. The R:R is 0.8:1 (observed) — negative risk/reward to the nearest resistance at $161.92. The defense AI spending surge from the Iran war is genuine: the $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (not firm revenue) and $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) are confirmed catalysts, and Motley Fool noted the Pentagon designated Maven Smart System as a program of record. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200 and Daiwa upgraded to Buy with $180 PT per the catalyst notes. However, the PEG of 3.62, near-zero FCF yield (0.36%), and 78x forward multiple mean you are paying a steep price for defense AI optionality. Revenue growth at 70% is strong, and the 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B (+61% YoY) per catalyst notes is impressive — but the 200-day SMA of $164.08 sits above current price, meaning PLTR is below all major averages. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI story is compelling, but 78x forward PE with 0.8:1 R:R makes this an avoid until the stock either pulls back to the $126-130 support zone or demonstrates a sustained SMA50 reclaim with volume.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Would reconsider to WATCH above $128 (50-day SMA vicinity) with volume confirmation — RSI at 28.8 is oversold and a bounce from these levels is plausible if the market rally sustains.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster is in a mixed transition: DBS stabilised at HOLD, UOB upgraded from CAUTION to HOLD as it approaches its 50-day SMA, while SIA remains CAUTION on the earnings decline concern. OCBC's overbought extension (RSI 71, 5.9% above SMA50) forces a downgrade from WATCH to HOLD with a hard entry block. The structural NIM compression narrative (day 22) remains unresolved — US10Y at 4.31% is still above the 4.20% reset threshold for bank earnings recovery.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — no reset signal yet
singapore.key_development: UOB's return to 50-day SMA vicinity is the most constructive technical development in the Singapore cluster this session, though no entry gates are yet satisfied.

semiconductors.summary: The semis cluster staged a powerful single-session bounce (SOXX +7.2%) on war de-escalation hopes, enabling NVDA and AMD to clear ACCUMULATE thresholds for the first time since TurboQuant disruption began on March 27. The cluster remains technically broken — every major name sits below its declining 50-day SMA — but today's volume-confirmed moves in NVDA and AMZN suggest fear may be peaking. ASML earnings on April 15 is the next major signal test for the entire cluster; MU's 16% SMA50 discount with deteriorating R:R (derived quality) keeps it in CAUTION.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Tactical bounce within structural downtrend — ACCUMULATE entries opened for NVDA/AMD
semiconductors.key_development: SOXX +7.2% reclaims some TurboQuant losses, with CoreWeave's $8.5B GPU-backed financing (per Yahoo Finance) providing concrete evidence that AI hardware demand has not collapsed.

big_tech.summary: BigTech cluster diverges further: AMZN's symmetric 1.0:1 R:R and GOOG's 0.2:1 R:R both argue for avoidance despite today's broad rally, while PLTR remains blocked on 78x valuation with a negative R:R. GOOG's downgrade to CAUTION reflects the asymmetric risk profile — the TurboQuant story creates a unique company-level tension where Google benefits from TPU cloud demand while disrupting the GPU ecosystem that supports most of the rest of the watchlist.
big_tech.thesis_status: Cautious — three CAUTION signals, no actionable entries
big_tech.key_development: Amazon's Kuiper satellite deal with Delta (per Yahoo Finance) and OpenAI's $122B raise (per AFP, with Amazon as participant) add cloud growth optionality but do not change near-term technical setups.

photonics.summary: Photonics cluster splits today: COHR upgraded from CAUTION to HOLD as it pulls back to its 50-day SMA, while LITE remains hard-blocked at 18.8% extension despite a +10.76% bounce. Both NVIDIA investments ($2B each, confirmed per NVIDIA newsroom) are intact, and Lumentum's Greensboro fab expansion validates the domestic manufacturing thesis — but the distorted R:R in COHR and extreme extension in LITE mean neither is actionable today.
photonics.thesis_status: Thesis intact, entries blocked — COHR approaching actionable zone
photonics.key_development: Coherent's pullback to the 50-day SMA is the most significant development — one more constructive session with volume could open a clean entry for the first time since the NVIDIA investment announcement.

energy.summary: XLE remains HOLD with a hard entry block at 11.6% above its 50-day SMA and RSI 71. Multiple MT Newswires reports on March 31 showed energy stocks fell in afternoon trading despite early strength — consistent with profit-taking at extended levels as Hormuz de-escalation hopes build. WTI at $103 is holding the structural geopolitical premium, but XLE's overbought condition means the trade is crowded.
energy.thesis_status: Structurally elevated but overbought — no entry
energy.key_development: Trump's announcement of coalition talks to protect Strait tankers (per CNBC reporting) is the most important de-escalation signal; if confirmed, XLE's premium could compress quickly.

index.summary: VUAA.L (S&P 500 accumulation ETF) sits at RSI 28.8 — deeply oversold — and 5.3% below its 50-day SMA, offering a potential contrarian entry if today's SPY rally (+3.1%) sustains. The tight invalidation at $121.62 (1.9% below) means normal volatility could trigger a stop, so structural support at $118.86 (4.1% below) should be used as the mental stop.
index.key_development: Today's SPY +3.1% broad rally on de-escalation hopes is the first potential reversal signal after 23 days of the S&P 500 technical breakdown narrative.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC)
interconnected.reason: NVDA and AMD ACCUMULATE signals both depend on the Blackwell Ultra and MI450 production ramps — TSMC fabs these chips, so TSMC utilisation and margin guidance directly confirm or deny the AI hardware demand thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if TSM pulls back 5-7% from current levels, or after ASML's April 15 earnings confirm High-NA EUV ramp pace.

interconnected.name: Broadcom
interconnected.reason: Broadcom's custom ASIC revenue (Google TPU, Meta MTIA) is the primary read-through for whether TurboQuant-style algorithmic efficiency genuinely displaces merchant GPU spending — a Broadcom revenue beat or guidance raise would validate the 'distributed AI silicon' thesis that threatens NVDA.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Broadcom's next earnings report for custom silicon revenue growth rate as the clearest signal on GPU vs. ASIC architecture shift.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: SK Hynix is the dominant HBM3E supplier alongside MU — if MU's TurboQuant-driven selloff is overdone, SK Hynix's order book data (reported in KRW) provides the independent confirmation that AI memory demand is intact.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes interesting if MU's RSI drops below 30 and SK Hynix shows no corresponding order cancellations in their next guidance update — use MU's technicals as the proxy trigger.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz crisis (day 23): Strait of Hormuz closure persists with minimal vessel traffic; Iran's Revolutionary Guard commander confirmed closure with threat to set vessels on fire, per CNBC.

US-China semiconductor and tariff escalation: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150 days) active; China retaliated with 34% tariffs and export controls on rare earths, per White House statements and Reuters/SCMP.
Google TurboQuant algorithm disruption: Paradigm shift risk to GPU-centric hyperscaler architecture announced March 27; AI capex thesis under sustained pressure.
Fed higher-for-longer rate environment: Rates held at 3.50%-3.75% per CNBC March 2026; sticky inflation constraining tech multiples with NFP due April 3.
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz crisis keeps WTI structurally elevated near $103, supporting XLE's overbought extension while compressing SIA (C6L_SI) earnings via fuel costs. The TurboQuant disruption has driven SOXX down approximately 18% from its pre-announcement peak, keeping NVDA, AMD, ASML, MU, and the photonics cluster in CAUTION or HOLD despite today's partial bounce — though NVDA and AMD have now cleared ACCUMULATE thresholds on improved R:R.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump announced coalition talks to protect tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, per CNBC — first concrete de-escalation signal in 23 days, contributing to today's broad market rally.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Selective starter positions in oversold quality names (NVDA, AMD) where R:R has cleared 2:1, while avoiding extended or fundamentally impaired names — hold cash reserves given unresolved Hormuz and TurboQuant risks.

events_this_week.event: CPI Report (March) — key test for Fed higher-for-longer thesis; a soft print could unlock rate cut expectations and re-rate tech multiples
MAS Monetary Policy Statement (April) — Singapore bank NIM trajectory dependent on MAS rate stance; could upgrade or downgrade the Singapore cluster
ASML Q1 2026 Earnings — sole EUV manufacturer quarterly print; could resolve CAUTION signal and upgrade entire Semis cluster if orders confirm AI capex thesis
Intel and Google earnings (same day) — Intel 18A customer commitment proof; Google TPU/TurboQuant architectural clarity; both binary thesis tests

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: TurboQuant algorithm disruption and Hormuz supply shock remain unresolved, keeping 8 of 17 tickers in CAUTION — but today's SOXX +7.2% bounce and CoreWeave financing have opened the first ACCUMULATE signals (NVDA, AMD) since the March 27 selloff began.

on_deck.note: RSI 36.6 approaching oversold; 3.4x forward PE if earnings growth thesis holds. Technically broken but valuation extreme.
